state_enterprise_profits.name: Profits from State Enterprises and Transaction Taxes

strategic_exports.name: Heavy Industry and Special Commodity Exports

national_defense.name: National Defense and Strategic Capability Enhancement

2026.methodology: The 2026 forecast applies a CAGR of 3.4% to the 2025 GNI estimate. Income is projected based on the intensification of the '20x10 Regional Development Policy' and expanded heavy industry output. Spending reflects a shift toward increased defense allocation (16.2%) as signaled in the 9th Party Congress (Feb 2026) and sustained investment in 'Economic Construction' which serves dual-use military and civilian industrial recovery. USD conversion is based on a projected stable parity against estimated shadow market rates and official SPA budget growth targets of ~1.8% in local currency terms.

state_enterprise_profits.name: Transaction Taxes and State Enterprise Profits

2025.methodology: The 2025 figures are calculated by applying the official 1.7% revenue growth target and 1.8% expenditure increase announced by the Supreme People's Assembly to the base GNI estimates. The total USD value is derived using a constant conversion rate from the estimated 47.9 trillion KPW budget. Defense spending reflects the official '15.9%' figure, though external analysts (SIPRI/BOK) suggest actual military-related industrial investment is embedded within 'Economic Construction' categories.

sources: Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) State Budget Report

social_insurance_rent.name: Real Estate Rent and Social Insurance

sources: Bank of Korea (BOK) Economic Growth Reports

2024.methodology: The total income is derived from the Bank of Korea's nominal Gross National Income (GNI) estimate for 2024, valued at 44.4 trillion KRW and converted to USD using the average 2024 exchange rate. Spending is calculated based on the historic state policy of maintaining a balanced budget with a slight surplus, applying the official percentage allocations reported by the KCNA during the Supreme People's Assembly sessions. Economic sectors (Industry, Mining, Construction) saw growth due to increased military-industrial cooperation with Russia.
